commonProblems.h2: Peugeot iOn Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot iOn's electric powertrain is generally robust, but three issues dominate replacement enquiries: Motor Control Unit (MCU) failure on 2011-2012 models, Transmission Control Unit (TCU) faults causing grinding or limp mode, and high-voltage errors often triggered by the 12V auxiliary battery. Every rebuilt motor supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Motor Control Unit (MCU) Failure - Why Early Peugeot iOn Models Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot iOn Active, Access, Allure (2011-2012, Y4F1 motor with MCU-001)
problems.rootCause: Internal power electronics failure in the inverter module, exacerbated by thermal cycling and known manufacturing defect covered by recall ZZZ.

repairOptions.tier: Diagnostic & Software Reset - Check for fault codes and attempt reprogramming
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD2 diagnostic scan, software update attempt
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if failure is software-related; temporary fix for hardware faults

repairOptions.tier: MCU Replacement - Reconditioned Unit - Swap faulty control module
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old MCU, install tested reconditioned unit with updated firmware
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full function; 12-month warranty covers recurrence

repairOptions.tier: Full Drive Unit Replacement - Motor + MCU assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace entire electric motor and control unit assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Most comprehensive solution; recommended for high-mileage vehicles

problems.recommendation: For 2011-2012 iOn models showing MCU-related faults, a reconditioned MCU replacement offers the best value. Given the iOn's low residual value (£2,000-£3,500), spending over £1,500 on repairs warrants careful consideration against vehicle worth.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt MCU prices for your Peugeot iOn

problems.h4: Transmission Control Unit (TCU) Failure - Grinding Noises & Emergency Mode
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot iOn all variants (2010-2017, Y4F1 motor with TCU-001)
problems.rootCause: Solenoid wear or electrical connector corrosion within the TCU housing, leading to incorrect gear ratio signalling and limp mode activation.

repairOptions.tier: Connector Inspection & Cleaning - Address corrosion issues
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove TCU connectors, clean contacts, apply dielectric grease
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective first step; may resolve intermittent faults

repairOptions.tier: TCU Replacement - Rebuilt Unit - Swap faulty control module
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old TCU, install rebuilt unit with calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores normal transmission function; 12-month warranty included

repairOptions.tier: Reduction Gear Assembly Replacement - Full drivetrain repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace TCU and reduction gearbox as integrated unit
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for vehicles with mechanical grinding noises

problems.recommendation: Start with connector inspection if symptoms are intermittent. For confirmed TCU failure with grinding noises, a rebuilt TCU replacement offers the best balance of cost and reliability for the iOn's value proposition.
problems.cta: Get quotes for TCU replacement on your Peugeot iOn

problems.h4: High-Voltage System Errors - When the 12V Battery Triggers Major Faults
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot iOn all variants (2010-2017, Y4F1 motor)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage (often unrelated to main battery age)
problems.rootCause: The 12V auxiliary battery powers control systems; when it fails or voltage drops, the high-voltage contactors may not engage, causing "engine shuts off" errors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace auxiliary battery, reset HV system codes
repairOptions.longevity: Often resolves "false positive" HV errors; first diagnostic step

repairOptions.tier: HV Contactor Replacement - Fix high-voltage switching
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty high-voltage contactors in battery junction box
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses genuine HV switching failures; requires specialist equipment

repairOptions.tier: Battery Management System (BMS) Replacement - Control module repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace BMS module controlling HV system safety
repairOptions.longevity: For persistent HV errors after 12V and contactor checks

problems.recommendation: Always test and replace the 12V auxiliary battery first when encountering HV errors on the iOn. This £95 fix resolves many "expensive" fault codes. Only proceed to HV component replacement if errors persist after 12V replacement and system reset.
problems.cta: Diagnose HV errors on your Peugeot iOn with UK specialists

problems.h4: Ignition Error Terminal 15 - Corroded Connectors Preventing Start
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot iOn Active, Access (2010-2013, Y4F1 motor)
problems.rootCause: Water ingress corroding the ignition positive (Terminal 15) connector, interrupting power to motor control systems.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean and reseal Terminal 15 connector
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective fix for early-stage corrosion issues

repairOptions.tier: Wiring Harness Section Replacement - Replace damaged section
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cut out corroded wiring, splice in new section with proper sealing
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution for severe corrosion; prevents recurrence

repairOptions.tier: Full Harness Replacement - Complete electrical repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace entire ignition circuit harness assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for vehicles with multiple electrical faults

problems.recommendation: Terminal 15 errors are often a simple, low-cost fix. Start with connector inspection and cleaning. If corrosion has damaged wiring insulation, a section replacement prevents future failures without the cost of a full harness.
problems.cta: Fix ignition errors on your Peugeot iOn today

problems.group: Don't Let Electric Motor Failure Write Off Your Peugeot iOn
problems.h4: Don't Let Electric Motor Failure Write Off Your Peugeot iOn

faq.h2: Peugeot iOn Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Peugeot iOn electric motor?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot iOn electric motors typically cost £800-£2,000 supply-only, depending on whether you need the full Y4F1 motor unit or just the MCU/TCU control module. Supply & fit adds £300-£600 labour.
keyPoints: Full Y4F1 motor rebuild: £800-£2,000 supply-only
MCU replacement only: £600-£1,500 supply-only
TCU replacement only: £700-£1,800 supply-only
High-voltage certified fitting adds £300-£600 labour
All rebuilt units include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Peugeot iOn motor replacement costs today

items.question: Is the Peugeot iOn motor control unit recall still valid?
items.answer: Yes, recall ZZZ for MCU failure on 2011-2012 Peugeot iOn models remains active. Contact Peugeot with your VIN to check eligibility for free repair or replacement.
keyPoints: Recall ZZZ affects MCU-001 units on 2011-2012 iOn models
Symptoms: loss of power, inability to restart or charge
Free repair/replacement available through Peugeot dealers
If out of warranty, rebuilt MCU costs £600-£1,500 supply-only
Always verify recall status before paying for MCU repair
items.cta: Check if your Peugeot iOn qualifies for free MCU repair

items.question: What causes the Peugeot iOn to show high-voltage errors?
items.answer: Most HV errors on the iOn are triggered by the 12V auxiliary battery failing, not the main traction battery. Replacing the £95 12V battery often resolves the fault without expensive HV repairs.
keyPoints: 12V auxiliary battery powers HV system control electronics
Low 12V voltage prevents HV contactors from engaging
Replace 12V battery first before diagnosing HV components
Genuine HV faults require specialist diagnostic equipment
HV system work requires IMI Level 3 certified technicians
items.cta: Diagnose HV errors on your Peugeot iOn with UK specialists

items.question: Can I replace the Peugeot iOn motor myself?
items.answer: No. Peugeot iOn high-voltage systems operate at over 300V DC and require IMI Level 3 certified technicians. Attempting DIY repairs risks severe electric shock and voids warranties.
keyPoints: HV systems exceed 300V DC - lethal voltage level
Legal requirement for certified technicians to work on HV systems
Specialist tools required for safe HV isolation and testing
Insurance may be invalidated by unqualified HV work
EngineMarket connects you with certified EV repair specialists
items.cta: Find certified Peugeot iOn motor fitting specialists near you

items.question: How long does a Peugeot iOn electric motor last?
items.answer: The Y4F1 motor itself often exceeds 100,000 miles with minimal maintenance. Most failures involve control electronics (MCU/TCU) or the 12V auxiliary system, not the motor windings.
keyPoints: Motor windings typically last 100,000+ miles with proper cooling
MCU/TCU control units are the most common failure points
Regular 12V battery replacement prevents false HV errors
Coolant system maintenance critical for motor longevity
Rebuilt motors with upgraded components can extend service life
items.cta: Get quotes for long-life rebuilt Peugeot iOn motors

items.question: Is it worth replacing the motor on a low-value Peugeot iOn?
items.answer: With iOn values typically £2,000-£3,500, spending £1,500+ on motor repair requires careful consideration. For reliable daily transport, a rebuilt motor with warranty often makes sense versus scrapping.
keyPoints: Compare repair cost against vehicle market value
Rebuilt motors with 12-month warranty offer peace of mind
Consider total cost of ownership versus alternative transport
Specialist repair costs are far below main dealer pricing
EngineMarket's comparison tool helps find best-value options
items.cta: Compare repair vs replace options for your Peugeot iOn

items.question: What's the difference between MCU and TCU failure on the iOn?
items.answer: MCU failure causes loss of power or charging capability; TCU failure causes grinding noises, slipping or emergency mode. Both require specialist diagnosis but have different repair approaches.
keyPoints: MCU: controls power delivery from battery to motor
TCU: manages single-speed reduction gearbox engagement
MCU faults often trigger charging system errors
TCU faults manifest as mechanical grinding or limp mode
Both control units are serviceable separately from main motor
items.cta: Diagnose whether your iOn has MCU or TCU failure

items.question: Does the Peugeot iOn share motors with Mitsubishi or Citroën?
items.answer: Yes. The iOn, Mitsubishi i-MiEV and Citroën C-Zero share the identical Y4F1 motor platform, MCU and TCU components. Parts are cross-compatible across all three models.
keyPoints: Joint venture platform developed by Mitsubishi Motors
Y4F1 motor code identical across iOn, i-MiEV and C-Zero
MCU and TCU part numbers cross-reference between brands
Specialist suppliers can source compatible parts from any model
Always confirm variant compatibility when ordering replacement parts
items.cta: Find cross-compatible Peugeot iOn motor parts today

items.question: How do I find the motor code on my Peugeot iOn?
items.answer: The Y4F1 motor code is stamped on the motor housing near the rear mounting point. MCU/TCU codes are on labels attached to the control unit casings. Your VIN can also decode the exact specification.
keyPoints: Motor code Y4F1 visible on motor housing rear face
MCU/TCU codes on white labels attached to control units
DVLA check via registration confirms variant and specification
Specialist suppliers can cross-reference VIN to exact parts
Take clear photos of codes when requesting quotes
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot iOn motor code instantly with registration check

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Peugeot iOn motor?
items.answer: All rebuilt motors supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Some specialists offer extended 24-month coverage for an additional cost.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 12 months unlimited mileage
Covers parts and labour for warranty claims
Some suppliers offer 24-month extended coverage
Warranty valid at any UK garage, not just fitting specialist
Always confirm exact terms before completing purchase
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Peugeot iOn rebuilt motors
